comment_text.sn35.26:1.2: MS edition omits sabbaṁ by mistake; it is found in PTS and BJT editions, and is included in the mirror passage below (SN 35.26:2.2).

root_text.sn35.26:1.1: “Sabbaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.2: Kiñca, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya?
root_text.sn35.26:1.3: Cakkhuṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.4: Rūpe anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.5: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.6: Cakkhusamphassaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.7: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya …pe…
root_text.sn35.26:1.8: jivhaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.12: yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.14: manaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.18: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:1.19: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.1: Sabbañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.2: Kiñca, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya?
root_text.sn35.26:2.3: Cakkhuṁ, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.4: Rūpe abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.5: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.6: Cakkhusamphassaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.7: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya …pe…
root_text.sn35.26:2.8: jivhaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.12: yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.14: manaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.18: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.26:2.19: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.26:1.1: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding the all, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:1.2: And what is the all, without directly knowing and completely understanding which, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering?
translation_text.sn35.26:1.3: Without directly knowing and completely understanding the eye, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:1.4: Without directly knowing and completely understanding sights …
translation_text.sn35.26:1.7: painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:1.8: Without directly knowing and completely understanding the ear … the nose … the tongue …
translation_text.sn35.26:1.14: the mind, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:1.15: Without directly knowing and completely understanding ideas …
translation_text.sn35.26:1.18: painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:1.19: This is the all, without directly knowing and completely understanding which, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:2.1: By directly knowing and completely understanding the all, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:2.2: And what is the all, directly knowing and completely understanding which, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering?
translation_text.sn35.26:2.3: By directly knowing and completely understanding the eye …
translation_text.sn35.26:2.8: the ear … the nose … the tongue …
translation_text.sn35.26:2.14: the mind, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:2.15: By directly knowing and completely understanding ideas …
translation_text.sn35.26:2.18: painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.26:2.19: This is the all, directly knowing and completely understanding which, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”
